title: 10 best herbal essential oils for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for jet lag are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation, caused by traveling across different time zones. The best herbal essential oils for jet lag include Lavandula angustifolia, Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Zingiber officinale, Avena sativa, Ginkgo biloba, Matricaria chamomilla, Anthemis nobilis, and Artemisia dracunculus. If left untreated, jet lag can lead to decreased productivity, irritability, and strained relationships, ultimately affecting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with jet lag because of its calming and soothing properties. When applied topically or inhaled, it can help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. Lavender oil's ability to regulate sleep patterns and promote relaxation can assist in adjusting to new time zones more quickly, allowing for a smoother transition into the local schedule. Its gentle aroma can also calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite the unfamiliar surroundings.
remedy_properties: Relaxation and sedative properties: English Lavender essential oil helps to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and adjust to new time zones.
Adaptogenic properties: This oil helps the body adapt to stress and changes in environment, reducing the symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, anxiety, and insomnia.

Anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties: English Lavender essential oil can help reduce physical discomforts associated with long flights or travel, such as headaches, muscle tension, and joint pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: English lavender roots are known for their calming and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in linalool, a compound that helps regulate the body's internal clock, promoting a sense of calmness and reducing fatigue, common side effects of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from your garden or purchase dried ones for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a warm air circulation oven at 60 degrees fahrenheit for four hours.
Add two cups of dried lavender flowers to a glass jar and pour in one cup of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for six to eight weeks stirring every three days for maximum extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids before storing the essential oil in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with jet lag because of its unique ability to regulate sleep patterns. The calming properties of lemon balm help to slow down a racing mind, promoting relaxation and reducing stress caused by time zone changes. This allows the body to adjust to a new rhythm, making it easier to fall asleep at the local bedtime. Additionally, lemon balm's sedative effects can also help alleviate jet lag-induced fatigue, leaving you feeling refreshed and revitalized after a long flight.
remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative Properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness.

Digestive Soothing Properties: Lemon balm essential oil has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe digestive issues like indigestion, bloating, and cramps that may occur due to traveling across time zones.

Adaptogenic Properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil is known for its adaptogenic properties, which means it helps the body adapt to stress and changes in environment, such as jet lag-induced fatigue, disorientation, and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemon balm roots are used to create calming and sedative essential oils that help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: The delicate petals of the lemon balm flower contain sweet-smelling compounds like linalool, which promote relaxation and reduce stress caused by time zone changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn for optimal oil quality and aroma.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to prevent oxidation.
Add 5% of jojoba oil as a carrier oil to dilute the melissa officinalis essential oil to 10ml.
Store the prepared blend at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with jet lag because they promote a sense of calmness and relaxation, which can aid in readjusting to a new time zone. The sedative properties of valerian oil help regulate sleep patterns, making it easier to fall asleep at the desired local time. Additionally, its calming effects on the nervous system can reduce anxiety and stress caused by jet lag, allowing for a smoother transition to the new environment.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Calming Effects: Valerian essential oil has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduce symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and restlessness.

Adaptogenic Properties: Valerian essential oil has adaptogenic properties that allow it to adjust to an individual's specific needs, helping to alleviate stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.

Antioxidant Properties: Valerian essential oil contains antioxidants that can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel and time changes, reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which helps regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduces stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes that promote relaxation, reduce muscle tension, and ease symptoms of insomnia caused by time zone changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 20% jojoba oil in a ratio of 10:1.
Place the root mixture in a double boiler and steam distill for 2-4 hours or until the oil is extracted.
Filter the essential oil from any impurities using paper filters or cotton gauze and store it in dark glass bottles.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 24 hours before use to enhance its therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava essential oils helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality. The calming properties of kava oil help to regulate the body's internal clock, allowing travelers to adjust to new time zones more quickly. Additionally, kava's sedative effects can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness that often accompany jet lag. By reducing stress and promoting a sense of calm, kava essential oils can help individuals adapt to their new surroundings and get the restful sleep they need.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Kava essential oil's anxiolytic properties can help reduce feelings of anxiety and stress caused by travel-induced disruptions to the body's natural rhythms, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.

Sedative and relaxing effects: The sedative properties of Kava essential oil can promote relaxation and calmness, which is particularly helpful for people who struggle with insomnia or restlessness due to jet lag, allowing them to wind down and prepare for a good night's sleep.

GABA-enhancing activity: Kava essential oil has been shown to enhance the effects of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation. By increasing GABA levels, Kava essential oil can promote deeper, more restful sleep and reduce symptoms of insomnia.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sedative and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety caused by jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Known for their calming properties, rhyzomes help to regulate sleep patterns and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh piper methysticum leaves measuring 10 grams and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-9 days or use a food dehydrator on low setting.
Combine dried piper methysticum leaves with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5 weight by weight and steep for 14 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate essential oils from solids and discard solids.
Transfer the extracted essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with jet lag because of its unique blend of sesquiterpenes, which have a profound impact on our body's circadian rhythms. The oil's calming and balancing properties help to regulate the body's internal clock, reducing symptoms of fatigue, disorientation, and insomnia often associated with jet lag. Additionally, the oil's sedative properties can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and get a good night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Anti-Anxiety Properties: Ginkgo biloba essential oil has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.

Sleep-Promoting Properties: Ginkgo biloba essential oil contains compounds that have been traditionally used to treat insomnia and promote a restful sleep, which is crucial for readjusting to a new time zone.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginkgo biloba essential oil has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel and adjust to a new environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils for jet lag relief.

Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe anxiety and fatigue associated with jet lag, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from a mature tree in early autumn, weighing approximately 500 grams for extraction purposes.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours to remove excess moisture and preserve quality.
Combine one kilogram of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 liter of high-proof vodka in a large glass jar, sealing it tightly overnight for infusion.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds from the plant material.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with jet lag because of its natural calming properties, which can aid in regulating sleep patterns. When used aromatically, chamomile oil promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite the disruption caused by crossing time zones. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects on the mind and body can help alleviate symptoms of fatigue and disorientation associated with jet lag, allowing travelers to feel more refreshed and revitalized upon arrival at their destination.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Chamomile's anxiolytic properties help to reduce anxiety and stress caused by jet lag, promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body.
Sedative: Chamomile's sedative properties can help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep, which is essential for adjusting to a new time zone and reducing jet lag symptoms.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by long-distance travel, promoting overall health and well-being during periods of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils to alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: The delicate white flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel-related disruptions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning when they are fully open and fragrant.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or a warm air circulation method for 24 hours.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase their surface area for extraction.
Steam distill the powdered flowers in a clevenger apparatus with distilled water at a temperature of 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil, which is rich in apigenin and bisabolol, and store it in dark glass bottles for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort essential oils helps with jet lag because of its unique ability to regulate sleep patterns and reduce fatigue. The oil contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. When used aromatically or topically, St John's Wort essential oil can help adjust the body's internal clock to the new time zone, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep while traveling across different time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: St. John's Wort is known for its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and changes in environment, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce fatigue, improve mood, and alleviate symptoms of jet lag-related exhaustion.

Serotonin-boosting effects: St. John's Wort is believed to increase serotonin levels in the brain, which can help regulate sleep-wake cycles and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and daytime fatigue often experienced during jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and phenolic acids, which help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety symptoms associated with jet lag.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and hyperforin, which have a calming effect on the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress caused by travel disruptions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a stainless steel apparatus at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect 10ml of hypericum perforatum essential oil, filtering it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Dilute the essential oil with 80% carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, in a dark glass bottle.
Store the finished blend in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with jet lag because of its ability to stimulate the mind and body, promoting a sense of relaxation and calm. The citrusy scent of lemongrass oil can help reduce anxiety and stress caused by travel disruptions, allowing you to adapt quickly to new time zones. Additionally, its invigorating properties can help increase energy levels and improve mood, making it easier to adjust to a new routine.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Lemongrass oil helps the body adapt to new environments and rhythms, reducing stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.
Circadian-regulating effects: The oil's unique composition can help regulate the body's internal clock, promoting a sense of balance and synchrony with local time zones.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate symptoms like fatigue, headache, and muscle soreness often experienced during jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lemongrass roots help to calm and relax the body, aiding in recovery from jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Containing potent oils, lemongrass rhyzomes possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of fatigue and disorientation caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopopgon citratus leaves in the morning after dew has dried for maximum oil content.
Dry the leaves using shade drying or air circulation for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a still at a temperature of 100-105°c for 4 hours to release essential oils.
Collect and separate the essential oils from the distillate using a decanting funnel and a separating funnel.
Store the obtained cymbopogon citratus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime essential oils helps with jet lag because they can help regulate your body's natural circadian rhythms. The uplifting and refreshing scent of lime oil can stimulate alertness and energy, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. Additionally, the calming properties of lime oil can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which is common when traveling across different time zones.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lime essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe muscle and joint pain, which may occur due to travel-related fatigue or changes in environmental conditions.
Antiviral and antibacterial: Lime essential oil has antiviral and antibacterial properties that can help boost the immune system and reduce the risk of illness during travel.
Adaptogenic: Lime essential oil has a calming and uplifting effect, which may help regulate the body's natural rhythms and reduce stress associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant are used to create an essential oil that helps regulate sleep patterns and reduces fatigue caused by jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Lime rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as joint pain and swelling.
Barks: The barks of the lime tree have been used to create an essential oil that promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia x europaea flowers and leaves in the morning for optimal oil quality.
Dry the plant material using a warm air dryer or sun drying method for 24-48 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a small-scale distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the distilled water and store it in dark glass bottles with tight lids at room temperature.
Add 10 drops of tilia x europaea essential oil to a diffuser or mix with carrier oil for topical application before bedtime.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with jet lag because they possess natural adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's internal clock. When inhaled, sage oil stimulates the brain's limbic system, which is responsible for controlling our circadian rhythms. This can help synchronize our bodily functions with the new time zone, reducing the symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, disorientation, and insomnia. Additionally, sage's calming and grounding effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress associated with traveling across time zones.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage essential oil helps reduce oxidative stress caused by travel-induced fatigue, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
Adaptogenic properties: Sage oil has been shown to help the body adapt to new environments and rhythms, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia, irritability, and digestive issues.
Calming and sedative properties: The essential oil's calming effects can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety associated with travel, promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
Barks: Contain bioactive compounds that help regulate sleep-wake cycles and improve sleep quality.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves in early morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 100g of dried leaves with 500ml of water for 2-3 hours using a copper still.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove impurities.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and keep it away from direct sunlight.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jet lag with herbal essential oils is to use a combination of calming and invigorating blends. Apply lavender oil to promote relaxation and reduce stress after arrival, helping your body adjust to the new environment. In the morning, use peppermint or eucalyptus oil to stimulate circulation and boost energy levels. Additionally, drink tea infused with herbs like valerian root or chamomile to aid in a restful night's sleep.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal essential oils are circadian rhythm disorders, stress-related fatigue, and travel anxiety. Essential oils such as lavender, bergamot, and peppermint can help regulate the body's internal clock, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. They can be used in aromatherapy blends, massage oils, or inhaled directly from a cloth or handkerchief to alleviate symptoms of these ailments.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with jet lag because its calming and regulating properties can ease the body's transition to a new time zone. The oil's sedative effects can promote a restful night's sleep, essential for readjusting to a new circadian rhythm. remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and anxiety.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has sedative and calming effects, which can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and improve sleep quality, essential for recovery from jet lag.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate physical discomfort and fatigue associated with jet lag, allowing the body to adapt to the new time zone.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high concentration of calming and sleep-promoting compounds.
Leaves: Lavender leaves contain a high amount of linalool, which helps with relaxation and reducing stress caused by jet lag.
Stems: Lavender stems are sometimes used for essential oil production, particularly for their high linalyl acetate content, which can aid in promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal oil quality and freshness.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a distillation apparatus at a temperature of 40-60 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and ensure its purity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with jet lag because it promotes a natural sleep-wake cycle, often disrupted during air travel. The oil contains valerenic acid, which calms the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate jet lag symptoms. By inducing relaxation and improving sleep quality, Valeriana officinalis essential oils can help individuals adjust to their new time zone more efficiently, reducing the duration and severity of jet lag. remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps alleviate jet lag by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation due to its sedative properties.
Valeranone: Contributes to jet lag relief by modulating the body's internal clock and regulating sleep patterns.
Isovaleric acid: Plays a role in reducing the effects of jet lag by improving sleep quality and reducing stress levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used for making Valerian essential oil due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which contributes to its sedative and calming properties.
Roots: They contain valerenal, a compound that helps regulate sleep patterns and is often used to alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Roots: The essential oil extracted from Valerian roots is also used to promote relaxation and reduce stress, making it beneficial for individuals experiencing jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal oil quality.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours using a still.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or by decantation.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a cloth to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with jet lag because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind, reducing stress and anxiety associated with long-distance travel. remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high content of the active compound melissal.
Flowers: The flowers are used to create a more delicate and fragrant oil, often used to help with relaxation and calming effects associated with jet lag.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis flowers and leaves at dawn when the plant is at its peak oil content for maximum yield.
Dry the freshly harvested melissa officinalis flowers and leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis flowers and leaves at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours to extract the oil.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with jet lag because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind. remedy_recipe: Harvest mature flowers from passiflora incarnata plants at dawn to capture optimal botanical properties.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill dried flowers at 3% water concentration and 100°c temperature for 2 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillate by decantation within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron membrane filter to remove impurities immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with jet lag because of its adaptogenic properties, which help regulate the body's internal clock. remedy_recipe: Harvest mature zingiber officinale rhizomes in late summer when the plant is in full maturity stage.
Clean the harvested rhizomes by brushing off dirt with a soft-bristled brush and then dry them thoroughly.
Steam distill the cleaned rhizomes in a distillation apparatus at a temperature between 100 to 120 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a glass container and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and then store it in a dark glass bottle.

Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are used to make essential oils for jet lag, as they contain a mixture of flavonoids and terpenoids that help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, alleviating symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees on a dry sunny morning to ensure quality.
Steam distill the leaves in a distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours at 100-150 degrees celsius.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a filter paper to remove impurities and improve its clarity for 15 minutes.
Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed tightly and label it properly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla essential oils helps with jet lag because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil's active compounds, such as bisabolol and alpha-bisabolol, have been found to reduce stress and promote relaxation, helping to regulate the body's internal clock. Additionally, chamomile oil's anxiolytic and sedative effects can alleviate symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and restlessness commonly associated with jet lag. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, Matricaria chamomilla essential oils can aid in readjusting the body's circadian rhythms.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: Acts as a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and irritability by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Alpha-bisabolol: Exhibits sedative properties, contributing to a restful sleep and reducing the effects of jet lag on the body's internal clock, allowing for quicker adaptation to the new time zone.

Farnesene: Possesses anxiolytic and sedative properties, helping to calm the body and mind after a long flight, thus facilitating the recovery from jet lag by promoting a more natural sleep-wake cycle.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make essential oils as they contain high levels of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps with relaxation and sleep.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are another source of essential oils, which help to calm the mind and body, reducing symptoms of jet lag.
Stems: The stems of Matricaria chamomilla are also used to make essential oils, which have a balancing effect on the body's internal clock, promoting a restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 kg of matricaria chamomilla flowers when they are in full bloom and the temperature is below 30 degrees celsius.
Dry the harvested flowers in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to maintain their potency and aroma.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100-150 degrees celsius.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anthemis nobilis essential oils helps with jet lag because it has a natural adaptogenic effect, promoting balance and relaxation in the body. The calming and soothing properties of the oil can help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by time zone changes, making it easier to adjust to a new environment. By promoting a restful night's sleep and reducing fatigue, Anthemis nobilis essential oils can aid in the body's natural ability to recover from the physical and mental strain of traveling across time zones.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: Acts as an anxiolytic, helping to reduce stress and anxiety associated with jet lag, promoting a smoother adaptation to new time zones.
Bornene: Exhibits sedative properties, which can aid in improving sleep quality and duration, a crucial factor in recovering from jet lag.
Pinocamphone: Displays a mild sedative effect, helping to regulate the body's internal clock and promote relaxation, thereby facilitating the readjustment to a new time zone.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their calming and soothing properties, which help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are also used to create essential oils, which have a slightly different composition than flower essential oils and can be used for a more subtle, calming effect.
Stems: Stems of Anthemis nobilis are sometimes used for essential oil production, providing a woody, herbaceous scent that can be used to promote relaxation and reduce jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers at peak oil content typically between 10am to 2pm when temperatures are mild.
Steam distill fresh flowers at 100c temperature and 10mmhg pressure for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled oil in a clean glass container and separate from water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove impurities and ensure quality.
Store the anthemis nobilis essential oil in a cool dark place in airtight glass containers away from light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia dracunculus essential oils helps with jet lag because it promotes a sense of relaxation and balance, which can be disrupted by long-distance travel. The oil's calming properties, often attributed to its high levels of borneol and camphor, can aid in regulating the body's internal clock. Additionally, its adaptogenic properties help the body adjust to new time zones, reducing the symptoms associated with jet lag, such as fatigue and disorientation. This can lead to a smoother transition for travelers.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: Helps with jet lag by regulating the body's circadian rhythms and promoting relaxation due to its sedative properties.
Bornene: Assists in reducing jet lag symptoms by reducing stress and anxiety through its anxiolytic effects, which in turn helps the body adjust to the new time zone.
Camphene: Aids in alleviating jet lag by stimulating digestion and improving sleep quality, which is often disrupted during long-distance travel.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Artemisia dracunculus are used to make essential oils that help alleviate symptoms of jet lag due to their calming and adaptogenic properties.
Roots: The roots of Artemisia dracunculus are used to make essential oils that help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce the effects of jet lag.
Stems: The stems of Artemisia dracunculus are used to make essential oils that help improve sleep quality and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40g of fresh artemisia dracunculus leaves and stems in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the plant material at 70-80°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water by decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and particles.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting cap for up to 6 months.
